profile.name: William
profile.role_in_household: Primary client; tends to lead financial discussions and wants the plan to feel orderly and predictable.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate-to-high: comfortable with account types and cash-flow concepts, asks clarifying questions about taxes and withdrawal sequencing, and notices when numbers don’t reconcile.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate but preservation-leaning: willing to take some market risk if the downside is bounded; prefers guardrails (cash buffer, diversification) and gets uneasy with large drawdowns or opaque strategies.
profile.communication_style: Direct and detail-oriented; will quote specific figures when he remembers them, and will pause to confirm assumptions. Uses practical language, occasional hedging like "I think" or "if I’m remembering right," and will correct small inaccuracies politely.
profile.decision_making_style: Analytical and sequential: wants to review categories (income, expenses, assets, insurance) and then decide; prefers to sleep on big changes but will commit once the logic is clear.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously trusting: open to recommendations but expects transparency on fees, risks, and why a strategy fits capital preservation.
hidden_concerns: worries about health-related costs later in retirement
concerned about paying more tax than necessary and making an irreversible mistake

profile.name: Emily
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; participates actively but often lets William drive the agenda, stepping in when something feels too risky or too complicated.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate: understands the big picture and goals, but prefers plain-English explanations; may mix up terms (gross vs. net, premium vs. benefit) and thinks in round numbers rather than exact line items.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate with a comfort-first tilt: okay with some fluctuation if it supports stability, but becomes skeptical when investments feel illiquid or hard to explain; values peace of mind over optimization.
profile.communication_style: Warm and conversational; asks "so what does that mean for us month to month?" Uses ranges and approximations ("around," "roughly"), occasional filler, and will interrupt gently to bring the discussion back to practical impact.
emotional_tendencies: stress when conversations get too technical
reassured by steady income and clear summaries
profile.decision_making_style: Consensus-driven: wants to hear the recommendation, then confirm it aligns with their lifestyle and comfort level; prefers fewer moving parts and clear next steps.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Generally trusting once rapport is established: responds well to empathy and straightforward explanations; may disengage if she feels talked down to or overwhelmed.
hidden_concerns: concerned about maintaining independence and not becoming a burden if health declines
quiet worry about having enough accessible cash without feeling "cash-poor"
